2603.25051.title: Approaches to Analysing Historical Newspapers Using LLMs
2603.25051.paper_summary: This study presents a computational analysis of the Slovene historical newspapers \textit{Slovenec} and \textit{Slovenski narod} from the sPeriodika corpus, combining topic modelling, large language model (LLM)-based aspect-level sentiment analysis, entity-graph visualisation, and qualitative discourse analysis to examine how collective identities, political orientations, and national belonging were represented in public discourse at the turn of the twentieth century. Using BERTopic, we identify major thematic patterns and show both shared concerns and clear ideological differences between the two newspapers, reflecting their conservative-Catholic and liberal-progressive orientations. We further evaluate four instruction-following LLMs for targeted sentiment classification in OCR-degraded historical Slovene and select the Slovene-adapted GaMS3-12B-Instruct model as the most suitable for large-scale application, while also documenting important limitations, particularly its stronger performance on neutral sentiment than on positive or negative sentiment. Applied at dataset scale, the model reveals meaningful variation in the portrayal of collective identities, with some groups appearing predominantly in neutral descriptive contexts and others more often in evaluative or conflict-related discourse. We then create NER graphs to explore the relationships between collective identities and places. We apply a mixed methods approach to analyse the named entity graphs, combining quantitative network analysis with critical discourse analysis. The investigation focuses on the emergence and development of intertwined historical political and socionomic identities. Overall, the study demonstrates the value of combining scalable computational methods with critical interpretation to support digital humanities research on noisy historical newspaper data.
2603.25051.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究呈現了對斯洛維尼亞歷史報紙\textit{Slovenec}和\textit{Slovenski narod}的計算分析，這些報紙來自sPeriodika語料庫，結合了主題建模、大型語言模型（LLM）基礎的層面情感分析、實體圖可視化以及質性話語分析，以檢視在二十世紀之交，集體身份、政治取向和國家歸屬如何在公共話語中被表現出來。利用BERTopic，我們識別出主要的主題模式，並顯示兩報之間的共同關注和明顯的意識形態差異，反映出它們的保守天主教和自由進步取向。我們進一步評估四個遵循指令的LLM，以進行OCR劣化的歷史斯洛維尼亞語的目標情感分類，並選擇了斯洛維尼亞適應的GaMS3-12B-Instruct模型作為最適合大規模應用的模型，同時記錄了重要的限制，特別是在中性情感上的表現優於正面或負面情感。在數據集規模上應用時，該模型揭示了集體身份描繪中的有意義變化，某些群體主要出現在中性描述性語境中，而其他群體則更常出現在評價性或衝突相關的話語中。我們隨後創建了NER圖，以探索集體身份和地點之間的關係。我們採用混合方法來分析命名實體圖，結合定量網絡分析與批判性話語分析。該研究聚焦於交織的歷史政治和社會經濟身份的出現和發展。總體而言，這項研究展示了將可擴展的計算方法與批判性詮釋相結合的價值，以支持對嘈雜歷史報紙數據的數位人文研究。
2603.25051.author: Filip Dobranić et.al.
2603.25051.authors: Filip Dobranić, Tina Munda, Oliver Pejić, Vojko Gorjanc, Uroš Šmajdek, David Bordon, Jakob Lenardič, Tjaša Konovšek, Kristina Pahor de Maiti Tekavčič, Ciril Bohak, Darja Fišer
